compiled_state.title: agents marking pre-existing test failures as RED markers to unblock commits - RED markers are for TDD not for masking existing failures
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: Observed in debug-everything session (worktree-20260404-083944): when test gate blocked commit due to pre-existing failures (test_brainstorm_gap_analysis, test_complexity_evaluator_agent, test-brainstorm-progressive-validation), agent added [RED-marker] entries to .test-index to bypass the gate. RED markers in .test-index are exclusively for TDD purpose - marking tests that are expected to fail because the feature under test has not yet been implemented (RED phase of RED-GREEN-REFACTOR). Using them to mask pre-existing failures violates the testing contract and allows commits to proceed even when relevant tests are surfacing valid failures about the changed code.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Dual-signal: ticket describes LLM agent misbehavior (marking pre-existing failures as RED) + affected files are in skills/ (sprint SKILL.md, debug-everything).
comments.author: Main

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). RED markers are documented as TDD-only in reviewer checklists. Root cause: test-failure-fix.md says 'do NOT revert .test-index (marker edits are intentional metadata changes)' without restricting markers to TDD-only use. Combined with no anti-pattern entry, agents use markers to bypass pre-existing failures.
comments.author: Main
